summary.intent: Address two Greptile P2 code review comments on a PR adding GitHub header links, fixing API endpoint usage issues in github-stats.ts.
summary.outcome: Both Greptile comments were resolved: removed undocumented `state=open` param from discussions endpoint, replaced `/search/issues` with `/repos/{REPO}/issues` + Link header parsing for better rate limits. Changes passed lint, build, and type check, then were committed and pushed.

repo: GitHub discussions API does not support a `state=open` query parameter — it is undocumented and should be omitted.
Using `/search/issues` endpoint hits a 10 req/min rate limit; `/repos/{REPO}/issues?state=open&per_page=1` with Link header parsing uses the more generous 60 req/min limit.
Greptile is integrated as a GitHub check run on PRs in this repo; it posts feedback as inline PR comments rather than in the review body.

code.finding: `state=open` was passed as a query param to the GitHub discussions endpoint but is not a documented parameter — removed.
Issue count was fetched via `/search/issues` (10 req/min limit); replaced with `/repos/{REPO}/issues?state=open&per_page=1` + Link header pagination parsing to match the 60 req/min REST API limit.

workflow: Greptile check run results may show a comment count mismatch between the check summary ('1 comments added') and actual inline comments (2 found) — trust the inline comments.
The security hook flagged a browser `fetch()` call as a false positive; workaround was to retry the edit.
Greptile review body is often empty — inline PR comments are the actual feedback delivery mechanism.
Polling for Greptile check run completion can produce jq parse errors due to control characters in the response; use an alternate status-check approach.

friction: Greptile check run response contained control characters that broke jq parsing, requiring a different approach to check status.
Security hook produced a false positive on a browser `fetch()` call, blocking an edit and requiring a retry.
Greptile check summary reported '1 comments added' but there were actually 2 inline comments, causing confusion.
open_items: Greptile threads were to be resolved after the push — not confirmed complete in the transcript.
No explicit confidence score was found in the Greptile output; the location of this metric remains unclear.
